prompt:
Platform tooling. Our deploy-gate service needs to know which change requests got approved since it last looked, and I finally have time to do this right. The repo already has a working thin Table API reader (SnowTableClient — single page per call, envelope parsing, X-Total-Count, error envelope to SnowApiException with retryAfter) plus Json/ChangeRecord. That part works; leave its behavior alone. What's missing is the polling layer.

Before writing it: web-search and fetch the CURRENT official ServiceNow Table API docs (developer.servicenow.com / servicenow.com docs). I specifically want you to confirm how sysparm_display_value and sysparm_exclude_reference_link shape reference fields like assignment_group, and the encoded-query syntax (^ conjunctions, ORDERBY, > comparisons on sys_updated_on). Our pinned contract is docs/contract.json, provenance docs/official_sources.json — both protected. If current docs contradict the fixture, flag it in your summary and don't touch fixtures or tests. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final answer.

The feature — new classes ChangePoller and PollResult:

- new ChangePoller(SnowTableClient client, int pageSize, java.util.function.IntConsumer sleeper). The sleeper gets seconds; never Thread.sleep directly — tests inject a recorder.
- PollResult poll(String checkpoint): scan ALL change_request rows with approval=approved and sys_updated_on strictly after the checkpoint, ordered so offset pagination is stable, raw values with reference links excluded (docs/contract.json pins the exact query string and field projection — read it). Page through with the client until a short page.
- PollResult has public final fields: java.util.List<ChangeRecord> records, String checkpoint, boolean complete, String failure.
- Checkpoint discipline: advance ONLY after a complete scan (max sys_updated_on seen; unchanged if the scan matched nothing). A record updated exactly at the checkpoint must never come back again.
- Failure discipline: 401 -> failure=UNAUTHORIZED, 403 -> failure=FORBIDDEN, no retry. 429 -> call the sleeper with the Retry-After seconds and retry that page, at most 3 times, then failure=RATE_LIMITED. In every failure case: keep the records already collected, complete=false, and the OLD checkpoint — we must never skip changes because a later page 500'd/throttled.

TestMain.java is the acceptance harness (loopback fake instance, dummy creds; it also re-checks the existing client behavior, which must keep passing). Run: java TestMain.java. TestMain.java and docs/ are protected — don't edit them.

research.interface: ServiceNow REST Table API, /api/now/table (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of GET /api/now/table/{tableName}, sysparm_query/sysparm_fields/sysparm_limit/sysparm_offset, the result envelope, and Link/X-Total-Count pagination headers

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: sysparm_display_value (false/true/all, default false) and sysparm_exclude_reference_link (default false) semantics for reference fields, sysparm_limit default 10000, Accept header requirement, error status codes

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow has no official Java SDK for the Table API; the documented integration surface is REST, and java.net.http.HttpClient is the idiomatic zero-dependency client for a JVM service feeding change data into deployment tooling.
